edges.evidence: Both papers investigate the phenomenon of training AI models on their own generated data, with the 2023 paper extending the analysis to show how these autophagous loops lead to model collapse.
While the 2023 paper shows paraphrasing can evade AI text detectors, the 2024 paper demonstrates that simple techniques can bypass detectors, suggesting even basic evasion methods are effective.
The 2024 paper extends the 2023 ESL writing study by providing broader empirical evidence of detector bypass techniques across educational settings.
Zephyr builds on the principle-driven self-alignment approach by implementing direct distillation methods for LM alignment without requiring extensive human supervision.
RLAIF builds on DPO's direct preference optimization approach by replacing human feedback with AI-generated feedback for reward model training.
AlpacaFarm extends DPO's preference optimization framework to create a simulation environment for studying human feedback learning methods.
The 2024 paper extends the comprehensive ChatGPT survey by specifically focusing on human-computer interaction and psychological perspectives.
The biomedical NLP benchmarking paper extends the time series forecasting approach of using LLMs for domain-specific applications to biomedical text processing tasks.
The 2022 paper extends blockchain supply chain analysis specifically to circular economy applications within the agri-food sector.
RO-Crate builds on AiiDA's computational infrastructure concepts by extending reproducible workflow packaging to broader research artifacts beyond just computational workflows.
The agriculture blockchain paper extends the food supply chain visibility concepts to broader agricultural applications and rationale.
The 2020 agricultural systems paper builds on the earlier blockchain agriculture rationale by providing specific technical implementations and current system applications.
